Block.Para: An Act to amend the Food and Drugs Act (trans fatty acids)

This bill was introduced during the First Session of the 40th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the First Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
The purpose of this enactment is to amend the Food and Drugs Act to ban trans fatty acids in oils and fats intended for human consumption.
An Act to amend the Food and Drugs Act (trans fatty acids)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. Section 2 of the Food and Drugs Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Block.MarginalNote:
âtrans fatty acidsâ
Â« acides gras trans Â»

Para.change: âtrans fatty acidsâ means unsaturated fatty acids that contain one or more isolated or non-conjugated double bonds in a trans-configuration;

Block.Para: 2. The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:

Para.change: 5.1 (1) This section applies to oils and fats, including emulsions with fat as the continuous phase, that are intended for human consumption.

Block.MarginalNote: Content of trans fatty acids in oils and fats

Para.change: (2) No person shall sell to a consumer any oil or fat that contains more than 2 g of trans fatty acids per 100 g of oil or fat.

Block.MarginalNote: Content of trans fatty acids in other food

Para.change: (3) No person shall sell to a consumer any food that contains one or more ingredients in addition to oil or fat if the food contains more than 2 g of trans fatty acids per 100 g of oil or fat in the food.
(4) This section does not apply to naturally occurring amounts of trans fatty acids that are contained in animal fats.

Block.Para: 3. (1) During the period of two years after the coming into force of this Act, the references in section 5.1 to â2 g of trans fatty acidsâ shall be read as references to â5 g of trans fatty acidsâ.

(2) Products manufactured before the coming into force of this Act and products manufactured within a period of two years after the coming into force of this Act may be sold until the expiry of the âbest beforeâ date.

Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
